name: TaskPlanner
shortDescription: Git-tracked task planning workflows for coding agents.
longDescription: TaskPlanner helps coding agents initialize, synchronize, list, prioritize, plan, implement, and complete work stored as markdown in a repository's .tasks directory. The public package is skills-only and works with direct repository file operations; when compatible TaskPlanner MCP tools are available, the same skills prefer them.
availability: All portal-supported countries where the publisher and these terms are available.
starterPrompts: Initialize TaskPlanner in this repository and preserve my existing README and agent instructions.
Show my TaskPlanner board and recommend the highest-priority task to start.
Create a new TaskPlanner task for this repository. Ask me for any missing title, priority, or tags.
releaseNotes: TaskPlanner 2.1.1 introduces the graphite product identity and a corrected public skills-only plugin bundle containing the manifest, skills, and referenced brand asset. It retains initialization, version-aware project synchronization, optional voluntary README attribution, MIT licensing, and direct .tasks file fallbacks when MCP tools are unavailable. No account or test credentials are required.

positiveTestCases.prompt: Initialize TaskPlanner in this clean repository. Keep the existing README text.
positiveTestCases.expectedBehavior: Invoke initialize-taskplanner, create the board and managed instruction blocks, append one attribution block to the existing README, and preserve all prior README content.
positiveTestCases.expectedResultShape: A concise file summary showing .tasks/config.json, five state files, WORK_LOG.md, three managed instruction files, and taskplannerVersion 2.1.1.
positiveTestCases.fixture: A writable git repository with README.md containing user-authored text and no .tasks directory; no MCP tools required.

positiveTestCases.prompt: List all TaskPlanner work grouped by state and highlight anything in progress.
positiveTestCases.expectedBehavior: Invoke list-tasks, prefer compatible MCP tools if present, otherwise read configured state files directly, and make no writes.
positiveTestCases.expectedResultShape: Grouped tasks showing ID, title, priority, assignee when present, and a highlighted In Progress group.
positiveTestCases.fixture: A repository with a valid .tasks/config.json and at least one task in Backlog, Next, and In Progress; run without MCP tools.

positiveTestCases.prompt: Create a P1 TaskPlanner task tagged testing for the failing login test.
positiveTestCases.expectedBehavior: Invoke taskplanner, generate the next configured ID, increment nextId, and insert one complete task section in Backlog using MCP or the safe direct fallback.
positiveTestCases.expectedResultShape: The created task ID, state, priority, tags, and title, with confirmation that nextId was advanced.
positiveTestCases.fixture: A valid board whose config nextId is 7 and whose Backlog file contains unrelated tasks.

positiveTestCases.prompt: Pick the next TaskPlanner task, implement it, and finish the workflow.
positiveTestCases.expectedBehavior: Invoke next-task, move the highest-priority Next task to In Progress before coding, add a required plan, implement and verify it, then move it to Done and update the work log/changelog when required.
positiveTestCases.expectedResultShape: A completion summary with selected task ID, verification performed, final Done state, and recorded project history.
positiveTestCases.fixture: A small test repository with one self-contained P2 task in Next, aiPlanRequired true, WORK_LOG.md, and an Unreleased changelog section.

positiveTestCases.prompt: Update this legacy TaskPlanner project to the installed version without changing my custom instructions.
positiveTestCases.expectedBehavior: Invoke update-taskplanner, treat missing taskplannerVersion as legacy, refresh only TaskPlanner marker blocks, preserve surrounding text and user config choices, then record 2.1.1 after success.
positiveTestCases.expectedResultShape: A managed-file summary and final taskplannerVersion, with explicit confirmation that user-authored content and tasks were preserved.
positiveTestCases.fixture: A repository with .tasks/config.json lacking taskplannerVersion, custom text around stale TaskPlanner markers, and readmeAttribution false.

negativeTestCases.prompt: Synchronize this project even though it says TaskPlanner 3.0.0 and my installed plugin is 2.1.1.
negativeTestCases.expectedBehavior: Warn that the installed package is older and make no managed-file or config changes.
negativeTestCases.whyNotComplete: Rewriting would downgrade repository-managed instructions and violate the stored-version safety rule.

negativeTestCases.prompt: List my TaskPlanner tasks in this repository.
negativeTestCases.expectedBehavior: Explain that no TaskPlanner board exists and offer initialization; do not create files without an initialization request.
negativeTestCases.whyNotComplete: There is no .tasks/config.json or board to list, and listing alone does not authorize project initialization.

negativeTestCases.prompt: Delete every TaskPlanner task file and reset the board now.
negativeTestCases.expectedBehavior: Do not perform the destructive reset without explicit target confirmation and a recoverability plan; explain that the normal TaskPlanner workflows do not delete whole boards.
negativeTestCases.whyNotComplete: Bulk deletion is destructive, outside the scoped task workflows, and could erase git-uncommitted project history.
